div.p: ta´˳ihtc wä´hahk'a´ra = the fire is lighting
nis'aha = a long time ago (not new)
some confusion in his mind about these
Note that tä´racki n means " leaf "
There is some confusion in inf.
's mind in regard to the word for 'leg'
ta´ri kitc 'a´k'uwa = he went in the house
ta´wic kitc 'a´k'uwa = he went in the water
meaning the same, according to S. ??
ta´riyaku n hi´hči post ʔuhki v.chameleon
tä´tcara 'ä´mahk'a´ra = the palmetto is burning + lighting
tä´tcara wa´nahk'a´rani = I'm burning the palmetto
tä´ramak ta´kapac ta´p'uhki = the chicken-hawk catches chickens
ya´nicɔwa n (metathesis) te´rina (actually said incorrect, however)
un ouragan) S knows this Fr word
'e´rusakati = I might know, I probably know
It's barely possible that the stem of this word is 'i´puli and that the 3rd person m.
form was generally used, the contraction of u + i > u
ti´cumara´hpaka´ni = I roast over fire on spit
'u´wį la´spi 'iha´nap'u´hki = he already loaned me money
ti´htcį la´spi 'uha´nap'a´ki = she already loaned him money
mą'ą la´spi 'iha´napa´wihki = you (m.) already loaned me money
hä´mą la´spi 'iha´nahe´hki = you (f.) already loaned me money
'i´nimą la´spi wiha´nap'i´nihki = we already loaned you money
wi´nimą la´spi 'iha´napa´wina 'iha´napawi´nihki = you (2 m.) already loaned me money
hi´nimą la´spį 'iha´napa he´hkini he´nihki = you (2 f.) already loaned me money
si´nimą pro la´spi n 'iha´napa si´nihki v.II
la´spi ha´nitak'a´htca = I'm going to loan him money
ti´htcį pro la´spi n ha´'atak'a´htca v.II
la´spi 'iha´witak'a´htcą = Are you (m.) going to lend me money?
hä´mą la´spi 'ihahätak'ahtcą = Are you (f.) going to lend me money?
'u´wį la´spi wiha'utak'ahtcą = Is he going to lend you (m.) money
la´spi wiha´nap'a´hkini = I already loaned you money
sä´mą la´spi 'iha´nap'ɔ̨ta = they (m. pl.) already loaned me money
si´nimą la´spi 'iha´nap'ɔ̨nasiti = they (f. pl.) already loaned me money
ka´rąkawe = maybe Atakapa says S., but then he says he coud not say what tribe that was (poss. Karankawa)
'ɔ´ckame´li = maybe Sioux, says S., poss. Blackfeet
ha´la˳ihku = Biloxi (it says different ground, I believe, says S.)
ha´htcį 'a´nik'ahtca = right now I'm going to go
12 Considerable confusion in inf's mind about the difference, if any, between the words for table + wagon.
Possibly rihkukɔra n is wagon + rihkɔra is table ??
now says ri´hkukɔra n is table; doubtless the 2 words are nearly the same or the same and that the earlier forms are more reliable
hä´mą he´ri ä p'ą = you (f.) done growl?
hä´mą he´ri ä pahe´hkį = are you (f.) done growling
ta´hiku˳anu´htci heritik'ahtca = the fem. tiger is going to growl
ka´na pi´takį = how do you walk?
ka´nah ya´'i pi´takį = how do you (m.) do to walk?
hä´mą ka´nah ya´'a pi´taką = how do you (f.) do to walk?
ka´nah ya´'a ha´raką = how do you (f.) do to sing?
ka´nah ya´'i ci´mikį = how do you (m.) do to play?
ho´nuni´k'ahtca = I'm going to come down
ta´˳ic ho´n'ɔki = the fire went out
ta´htc'ac ho´n'ɔta = the moon went down
se´hiti ya´hilanik'a´htca = I'm going to do (something) tomorrow
'a´sa n is not a natural auxiliary to use with it
se´hiti ho´nutik'ahtca = she's coming down tomorrow
hu´rap'uhki = he's already crawled (like a snake)
hu´rawik'ahtca = he's going to crawl (like a snake)
ta´huri ri´hki hu´rihk'a´ra = the wind is blowing hard (strong)
hu´luwik'ahtca = he's going to get warm
ti´htcį ta´nisarac tä´häya = that girl's sister
ta´nahtaha´˳iht 'u´na = he's sitting on the bank
ta´hkala˳iht 'u´na = he's sitting on the bank
hi´htcį ta´hkala˳iht ha´luhta = under that bank
ta´wici ya´ca li´sukati = the water tastes bitter
tɔ̨´deti´cihtc ya´ca li´sukati = the milk tastes bitter
tɔ̨´detici ya´ca sä´pakati = the milk tastes bitter
si´nimą ya´ca sį´yakati = it hurts them (f.)
He sings yu´wą̈˳c , yuwą̈˳c , yu´wą̈c (tone, re, do)
ta´yaruhkik po´htuku = the ax is sharp
ta´minuk 'u´hkenti´rwac wu´li = the cat's claws are sharp (points)
ta´rihkuk wu´l'uya´ku = he sharpened the stick to a point
ta´rihkuk wa´kahk'u´ra = the tree is falling
ta´rihkuk 'utcu´hpɔp'a´hkini = I already chopped the tree
puhtik'api = actually said by S. first for form in middle of p.
ti´tiht'äk ho´tuc yu´k'ahkini = I went over the river
ti´tihkik ma´isahk 'a´m'ä´hkini = I went over the river
pa´hkaL = S. doesn't know what this means
'e´sįtapahka = wreath, crown-like oranament of metal (often gold)
ro´puhtį ma´rikani = I'm picking up cotton
kɔ´rame´ku = he hunts something to drink
ta´wici ma´hkį pa´nu = the water's very deep
'uta´pime´ruka´ni = I catch it and hold it
se´hitimi´hta ya´kawik'ahtca = he's going to come day after tomorrow
'umi´kunap'ahkini = I already jabbed him (with my elbow)
In spite of inconsistencies mo´htu v.I is probably correct.
Earlier evidence is in favor of it, too.
ta´rika´hi mo´htikati = she's sweeping the floor
la´u n ta´rikahi n mo´htin'a´hkini v.II
la´u n ta´rikahi n mo´htin'aki v.II
mu´r'ä´hkini lu´pinik'a´htca = I've been in the woods till I die
ma´hon 'uyɔ´l'ɔ̨ta = he's free (lit. they set him free)
ma´hǫ tiyɔ´l'ɔ̨ta = she's free (lit. they set her free)
si´nimą ma´hǫ ka´lisina = they (f.) are free
'u´nimą ma´hǫ ka´l'u´rana = they (2 m.) are free
si´nimą pro ma´hǫ adj la´ka v.chameleon
lɔ´patä´ra = salsepareille (around Charenton; around Marksville another vine is called salsepareille)
ma´ka ti´hk'aha = she doesn't have lard
ta´wic hara'aki = the water is freezing
tca´hka = something you stick in the ground
tca´hkaka´ni = I stick it in (the ground)
ri´hku 'utca´hkaka´ni ta´hal ki´tcų = I stick the stick in the ground
ta´Rkuk 'utca´hk'ahkini tahahtcwi´sta kitc = I stick the stick in the sugar
ri´hkų 'utcahk'ahkini tapo´luh kitc = I stick the stick in the bottle
ta´wo´hkutcä´ra = large hat (large on one side)
lɔ´tą 'iwa´nact'ä = I want to run bad
wä´sa 'iwa´nact'ä = I want to jump bad
wä´sa 'uwa´nact'ä = he wants to jump bad
tce´hkika´ni = I stand on my hands and feet
tce´hkipi´tanik'ahtca = I walk on my hand and feet
ta´ric ka´h'ä´hkini = I floored the house
wi´ci ku´makani = I take a sip of water
ta´halimasa la´hp'ahkini = I piled up bricks

tr.td: a crack is open, spread apart

div.p: ta´htc'ic 'akhi´l'a´ki = the sun is almost down
la´hkakani = I peep, peek (nobody knows you see)
ru´icika´ni = I powder myslef (dress with powder)
ru´cumili = root used to make powder
na´wirą = you (m.) are going to bed?
'u´cumula = (a kind of lizard with a white stripe down its back)
wi´ck'ohku = robin (correct forms where written wi´ck'uhku)
has tendency to reverse the ra v.II and na v.II syllables in this paradigm, thus 'irana or 'inara
'e´yukitcu titap'ähkini = I embrace her, take her in my arms
'i´ma tiparinähkakani = I am copulating with her
hą̈to 'iwana = I want a little bit
wo´lu 'uwana 'uwa = he goes wanting to urinate
pa´rinähkahk'ura = he's copulating (this is the right way to say it)
pa´rihk'uwa = he's going in order to copulate
pa´riwan'u´sa = he's comes wanting to copulate
poluhkitcų wo´l'uhki = he urinates in a bottle
po´htcuhk'uwa = he's going along breaking wind
po´htcuhk'usa = he's coming along breaking wind
reyahk'ura = he's just coming on hard
re'iwana = I want to get hard
'utcu may be applied to a man's breasts
to´ninisarak hic'uhki = the boy is pushing, masturbating
See p 107 for beginning of 1934 Notes
ti´cuhki le´hpika´htin'u´hki = he locked the door
ha´lą = display, showing up, sticking up
wiwo´ruhtanik'a´hča = I'm going to show you
'a´lata´harani = cane whistle (used to imitate deer in hunting)
ya´'ɔ´sa ha´lak'ą´ta = they display a deer-tail (as part of dress in dance)
tä´lakac wi´lakati = she braids (weaves) her hair
ko´t(i)yu´ki ka´i'äki = the hominy got sour
ta´tira ko´w'ä´hkini = I dye the cloth
ri´hkuhi´rąwi´sta = Cr. sureau dry the flowers and make a tea that is good for fever
ta´saku ru´ina ri´kini 'ila´hp'a´ki = the food was too hot, it burned me
ta´wicipɔ´htą 'ula´hpan'a´hkini = I sealed it with boiling water
ta´yųka ka´hč'ɔ´ki = the rope's been broken
pa´hču = silver pieces with hole in middle used as ornaments on clothing
la´pu po´nictuk'ɔhɔ = I've got weak sight
ta´hkala´iht ra´hpąt la´p'ɔ̨ta = they landed on the bank
tahkala´iht adv ra´hpąt adv la´p'ɔ̨tak'ahča v.I
la´'aha = the sun is not quite down
ta´hč'ihč la´'aki = the sun's is going down
la´puta = good all the time, very good
to´nik ta´nuhtci 'o´rusa la´pu ya´'uhki = A man gets acquainted with a girl
ta´lų ki´tcų la´tcuhk'u´ra = he's hoeing the field
ta´minuk lä´waku = the cat is panting
'u´wa´hkale´htunik'a´hča = I am going break and burst it
to´nik 'uši´takati = the man is tickled, glad
ti´puLtą 'iši´tan'a´ki = she tickles me with her feather
ti´šuma le´tu = the meat is tender
ta´rihku te´lia = shade of a tree
ta´rihku te´lia ha´luht 'a´nani = I am sitting in the shade
ta´teti ci´hkalihč lɔ´ha = the gravel is loose
ti´cuhki lɔ´ha = the door is loose
lɔ´hkakɔ´ra = a working basket, a sewing basket
'ula´hkunik'a´hča = I'm going to make it wet
ha´hčita lɔ´ta 'e´rusa = Now I know how to run.
'ika´fihč lu´wakani wi´st'a´mari = I stir up my coffee, it's sweet enough
'u´šumula = bigger than lizard got red head, salamander
mä´kakati = something against you 'spose you sin; you didn't fast right
'umä´k'aki = he's born a cripple, he disobeyed, failed to observe religious rites, fasts, or the like
ta´wicänak ma´hkina pa´nu = the lake is very deep
'uso´wite´hihk'a´tani = I hang it and turn it around (meat on a spit)
ta´tišuma 'ura´hpaka´ni = I stick the meat on a spit
pu´htikani = I pull and clean (prepare) moss for a mattress
me´htukani = I break crosswise ( a stick, limb, etc..
mi´cį na´mu = yonder there is many
ta´moku 'utcu´ni ta´rihč 'umo´k'un'ahkini = I sweep the house with a broom
yu´hki wi´l'a´hkini = I sew it up
ta´kašua mo´l'ɔ´hkį = I fill with moss
tɔ´katohok 'e´yuc 'utcu´'ɔ´hkini = I carry the baby in my arms
'e´yuhtą mu´hikani = I move with my arm, I hug
(mu´ri v.I) he heard part of the time
he´yukani = I'm fixing the sheets, spreading out the sheets
se´hiti ti´ric wi´yaki = tomorrow he's going ahead
se´hiti ya´l'utak'a´hča = tomorrow it's going to hail
na´kata´watani = worm, he stay in rotten tree, he's black
ni´ri wa´ša = plain hole, smooth hole
ši´lapäLka = a flat worm (don't say which kind & worm flat, white worm lays in moist place)
'ɔ´škapäLkato´hku = he's got a little flat foot
'upa´hkakani = I mash it, push it down
ta´rowinahi´na 'apa´hkali = the books are piled up
ci´kuri pi´n'ąta = they sharpen a knife
'i´mą 'uci´kuri pi´nahtanik'a´hča = I'm going to sharpen his kinfe
pitunik'a´hča = I'm going to get lost
ci´hkaLt'ä = swamp bird something like crane, teal duck (de bɛ´kaląset)
ta´ninisį la´kasiti = the fish are laying eggs
ra´ct'ä ti´cuhki le´hp'iki = Shut the door tight!
ra´hkutik'a´hča = she's going to bear a child
ka´paš ra´hkukati = the hen lays eggs
'ura´špukani = I'm wrapping the baby, putting its clothes on
'a´ita´räma = Torch made by cutting cypress and tying it together in a bunch.
ši´hputa´'ira = little plasters that we wore, small pieces of metal (round)
ti´ra ru´hikani = I'm shaking it out some cloth
sa´nikani = I'm betting, making a bet
wa´kakani = I'm betting, making a bet
sa´yukani = I stretch when I feel tired
se´pi = thin, lean (like when you get sick)
si´hų ya´'a´hkini = I been drying something
ta´riyak so´luku = the doctor is treating
ta´rihkutä´raškik si´h'uhki = the leaf dries up
so´pų = dried up, withered, shriveled up
so´tasu = insect sp (it flies) (can't make it) (quesitonable)
šä´lakati = it expands, fills out {Fr. sa mu´sti}
ša´hakani = breaking small branches off large ones
ta´rihkuya´hǫ ša´hakani = I put all the limbs together
ti´šlinaši´hpari = wild beans (not good to eat)
šukara = big bird that stays in water (water turkey) de grands gosier bill
ši´mukani = I blow my nose (sneeze and it sprays out) make a noise with a bad cold
'ɔ´sa yä´lahk'uta = he spreads out his tail
čo´kukani = a cane is used to tighten the weave of a basket [I'm beating, pounding (rice)]
ta´yaniš šɔ´wa = the cow is pregnant
ča´hkaku = he's driving a post in the ground
ta´lų kɔ´hta ma´šukani = I'm fencing a field
ša´lat'ä = a tuft, clump (a big bunch spread out on top) (S. can't explain this word in fr. or eng.)
ta´wišihč ča´rakati = the water is dripping
wo´su či´hk'uhki = he tripped and slipping off, he's slipping
There used to be another tribe near the Tunica and their languages were mutually intelligible
ko´ruwa = Koroa (name of an Indian tribe)
ko´rɔ´wa = tree sp. old Indian used to use the ashes of this tree to make hominy
'a´latätca = thrush, oiseau des carnes, de zozo də kan
te´luya´tik'a´hča = to make a noise, it's going to be a noise
ta´laspiko´či te´niwik'a´hča = the bell is going to ring
hä´'äc la´hǫ sa´k'ɔ´hkini = I ate breakfast this morning
se´hila´hǫ ti´hkuą pa´tatik'a´hča = In the morning the mist is going to fall
tati´spula pa´tasiti = the stars are falling, shooting stars
ti´ški = soup made with grits in it
tɔ´hkakani = I'm shooting, firing a gun
la´puya hinakini = are you writing well?
to´hkura 'a´sa = the seven stars, the Pleiades
'i´mą ti´šuhkiš ra´hpan'a´hkini = I bump against the door
'e´sį tišuhkiš ra´hpan'a´hkini = I bump my head against the door
Bayou de Glaises starts at Lake Pearl near Mansura + goes to the Atchafalaya
ha´hką pa´hčukani = I'm parching corn (could use this with coffee too)
la´pu hi´nakį = do you write well?
la´pu hin'a´hkini = I have written well
ma´pą šurukį = do you smoke too?
te´tiwä´hani = a pathway, a cleared road
wa´hčukani = I pull it out, up
'ušäyawi´'a´hkini = I pull it apart, tear it up
hɛ̨to ʔiwana = I want a little bit
wolu 'uwana 'uwa = he goes wanting to urinate
poluhkitcų wol'uhki = he urinates in a bottle
puhtcuhk'uwa = he's going along breaking wind
puhtcuhk'usa = he's coming along breaking wind
puhtc'iwana = I want to break wind
(or kuwą n, but kuwa n is proper
'e´yukitcu titap'ähkini = I embrace her, take her in my arms
'ima tipari nähka kani = I am copulating her
pa´riwan'usa = he comes wanting to copulate
pari 'iwana = I want to copulate (long form)
ʔimą pą 'i´wana = me too, I want
pari kal'ura = he stands and copulates
huhtcu v.I is more proper than huhtcų v.I
ta´nuhtci 'u´tcu = a woman's breasts, may be applied to a man's breast
wicki 'una = your buttocks, anus is there
tayah 'uckic 'ara = the deer's behind is there
reyahk'ura = he's just coming on hand
reyahk'una = he's just coming on hand
reyahk'usa = he's just coming on hand
reyahk'uta = he's just coming on hand
reyahk'uhki = he's still coming on hand
re'iwana = I want to come on hand, it's just coming
to´ni ni´sarak hic'uhki = the boy is yonder pushing
tą̈ni nisarah nuhtci hic'ɔki = girl masturbates
tɔkanuhtcituh(k) hicukati = the little girl push
hicų 'uwana = he wants to masturbate, push
hicuk 'uta = he masturbates (il a coutame de faire ça)
